title: Biden signs infrastructure bill, promoting benefits for Americans.
content: Billions of dollars will now pour into American communities, although the final package falls short of the president’s ambitions.
en_title: Biden signs infrastructure bill, promoting benefits for Americans.
en_content: Billions of dollars will now pour into American communities, although the final package falls short of the president’s ambitions.

title: Biden Got His Bipartisan Win. Now, Reality Sets In.
content: President Biden, who is facing low approval ratings, hopes the infrastructure bill will help him project sustained progress in confronting a surge in inflation and growing alarm about the midterm elections.
en_title: Biden Got His Bipartisan Win. Now, Reality Sets In.
en_content: President Biden, who is facing low approval ratings, hopes the infrastructure bill will help him project sustained progress in confronting a surge in inflation and growing alarm about the midterm elections.

title: Taiwan, Trade, Tech and More: A Tense Era in U.S.-China Ties
content: The Biden administration has called managing America’s relationship with Beijing “the biggest geopolitical test of the 21st century.”

title: Biden’s reliance on I.R.S. enforcement to pay for $1.85 trillion bill hits a snag.
content: The head of the Congressional Budget Office said on Monday that beefed-up tax enforcement would raise far less money than the White House projected.

title: Subway Fare Increase ‘Off the Table’ Thanks to Infrastructure Bill
content: Officials said that federal money, including the $1 trillion infrastructure bill, will help the M.T.A. keep fares stable for at least six months and avoid service cuts.
en_title: Subway Fare Increase ‘Off the Table’ Thanks to Infrastructure Bill

title: Beto O’Rourke Announces Run for Texas Governor, Testing Democrats’ Strength

title: Europe Toughens Rules for Unvaccinated as Fourth Covid Wave Swells
content: Austria took the hardest line yet on Monday, beginning a lockdown aimed exclusively at those who are not inoculated, part of a pattern to make life harder for resisters.

title: Four Lives Lost: Inside America’s Homicide Surge
content: In many cities, towns and rural areas, homicides are up in 2021, even after a sharp rise last year. The reasons are elusive. The impact is immeasurable. Here are four stories from one month that offer a glimpse of the toll.

more_info.byline: By Julie Bosman, Mitch Smith, Neil MacFarquhar, Tim Arango, Chloe Reynolds, Adria Malcolm, Mason Trinca, Cydni Elledge and Stephen Speranza
